title: Documentation, APIs, Licenses, and Generated-Artifact Gates · Gate Lens: Api Schema
summary: Keep human and machine contracts synchronized with implementation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates api schema increment in a documentation and contract drift suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate API schemas, generated clients, migrations, and compatibility changes.
Test README setup and core commands from a clean environment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Api Schema evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-api-schema-lxkhen: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Api Schema into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-api-schema-lxkhen:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Api Schema model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Api Schema applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate API schemas, generated clients, migrations, and compatibility changes.
